title: Melding Financial Operations and Analytics: The New Financial Battleground
author: Alex Veytsel
abstract: Alex Veytsel of Aberdeen Group argues that the Sarbanes-Oxley Act is accelerating convergence between Financial Value Chain Management (FVCM) and financial analytics software, creating a corporate performance management imperative. The insight categorizes three supplier heritage types—financial analytics vendors, FVCM specialists, and ERP companies—evaluating each against SOX compliance needs, with ERP vendors holding a trump card via general ledger control. Aberdeen predicts that innovative FVCM suppliers will either add analytic capability or be displaced, and that SOX-driven transparency will prove beneficial beyond compliance.
importance_rationale: Foundational Aberdeen analysis of the Sarbanes-Oxley compliance software market structure published within one year of SOX passage; first systematic vendor-type taxonomy for financial compliance technology.
relevance_rationale: The FVCM-analytics convergence thesis (now CPM/GRC markets) was largely vindicated; specific vendor names (MIS AG, Alterna, XRT) are dated but the supplier heritage taxonomy remains analytically useful.
prescience_rationale: Correctly predicted FVCM-analytics convergence that became the CPM and GRC software markets; ERP general-ledger trump card prediction vindicated by Oracle, SAP, and Microsoft Dynamics dominance; FVCM specialists did add analytics capability as predicted.
